mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes, with Rank IC remaining below 0.02 over all 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, severe labor market slack signals collapsing consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.5%, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal spending and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with a hit rate below 45% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of expansionary periods, invalidating the supply-demand transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls signal robust labor market growth and rising wage income, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day SPX direction, with cumulative abnormal returns indistinguishable from zero across all test periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 310.0 trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening and margin compression, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 58% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 310.0, or the signal information coefficient turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly real personal spending confirms broad-based economic demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth signal yields a negative hit rate for 20-day SPX returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during late-cycle peaks signal impending manufacturing contraction and inventory gluts, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day SPX returns in late-cycle environments, with a predictive accuracy rate below 48% over rolling 5-year windows.
